metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Each child story (08a8, 21dd, 36c7, 6168, fd84) has an AC item requiring that plan-review advisory findings are recorded with Accept/Reject disposition as a ticket comment and any Accepted findings are reflected in the description before the change's Gerrit LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1 votes.
findings.finding: Parent AC6 ('Each child story's plan-review advisory findings are recorded with an explicit Accept/Reject disposition and a one-line rationale as a comment on the child ticket, and every Accepted finding is reflected in the child's description before that child's change receives its Gerrit LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1 votes') is not encoded as a verifiable acceptance criterion in any of the five children. No child has an AC item requiring this disposition record, so the completion verifier has no per-child evidence to inspect and the gate can be bypassed silently.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a final AC item to each child story: '[ ] Plan-review advisory findings for this story are recorded as a comment on this ticket with an explicit Accept/Reject disposition and one-line rationale; every Accepted finding is incorporated into this description before the change receives Gerrit LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1 votes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Finding 2 child story exactly WHICH entry point(s) auto-write the store-format-version record (init/re-init, `fsck --repair`, MCP boot — consistent with the existing `run_ensures` call sites), and revise the 'next covered write' language to name those specific triggers so the implementer does not wire `run_ensures` into the hot `write_and_push` path.
findings.finding: Finding 2's plan states 'the idempotent ensure-registry writes the record on the next covered write' and that 'upgrade-in-place works with no manual step', but the existing architecture explicitly decouples the write-hot-path from ensure sweeps: `write_and_push` calls only `maybe_emit_pending_hint` (a nudge, never a repair), while `run_ensures` (which actually writes ensure units) fires only at init/re-init, `fsck --repair`, and MCP server boot. An implementer following the plan literally would either incorrectly wire `run_ensures` into the hot write path (violating the explicit hot-path budget design in `src/rebar/_store/ensures.py` and `docs/migrations.md`) or would silently leave the format-version record unwritten for CLI-only users who never hit one of those three entry points.
findings.location: Finding 2 / Context section ('the idempotent ensure-registry writes the record on the next covered write') and AC bullet ('record auto-written by the ensure-registry on next covered write')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the idempotent ensure-registry writes the record on the next covered write' with 'the idempotent ensure-registry writes the record at the existing `run_ensures` entry points (init/re-init, `rebar fsck --repair`, and MCP server boot)' — and update the AC bullet to match. This stays true to the existing hot-path budget design, is still 'no manual step' for the two most common upgrade paths (re-init or MCP restart), and avoids over-promising for CLI-only users.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] 21dd AC7 is amended to explicitly require that docs/migrations.md documents the absent-record (pre-v1.0 store) upgrade-in-place path and the rollback-safe outcome for a pre-v1.0 binary encountering the compatibility record (not just the fail-closed gate and deployment-order rationale).
findings.finding: Child 21dd-7a0b-2119-4473's AC7 enumerates what docs/migrations.md will document ('the compatibility record and the fail-closed gate, including the deployment-order rationale') but does not explicitly commit to documenting the rollback-safe outcome for a pre-v1.0 binary encountering the record. Parent AC5 requires docs/migrations.md to include 'the absent-record upgrade/rollback path,' and parent AC4 requires the Finding 2 story to document 'a rollback-safe outcome for a pre-v1.0 binary encountering the record.' The test/analysis obligation is in 21dd AC4, but the documentation location is not explicitly bound to docs/migrations.md.
findings.location: child 21dd-7a0b-2119-4473, Acceptance Criteria item 7 (docs/migrations.md) vs parent AC4 and AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Amend 21dd AC7 to read: 'docs/migrations.md documents the compatibility record, the fail-closed gate, the absent-record legacy/compatible policy (upgrade-in-place with no manual step), the rollback-safe outcome for a pre-v1.0 binary encountering the record, and the deployment-order rationale (why the reader ships in v1.0).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief procedural description in the plan body (e.g. under Context or a 'Process' subsection) that explains how plan-review advisory dispositions are recorded and verified, so AC item 6 has corresponding described work.
findings.finding: AC item 6 requires that each child story's plan-review advisory findings be recorded with an Accept/Reject disposition and reflected in the child's description before Gerrit votes — a non-trivial process step — but the plan body contains no described work, ownership, or procedural description corresponding to this requirement. It is an orphan AC with no covering described deliverable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 6

findings.finding: Sibling tickets touch the same file(s) with no ordering edge.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a depends_on/blocks edge to serialize, or partition the file ownership.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Child 6168 includes an explicit AC (or scope constraint) requiring that the build-isolation enforcement mechanism from 08a8 (--no-build-isolation flag or Hatchling pin in pyproject.toml [build-system] requires, plus the workflow lint/test step) is preserved intact after 6168's release.yml and pyproject.toml changes.
findings.finding: Child 6168-df06-1e08-4ea3 modifies the build invocation in release.yml and pyproject.toml without requiring preservation of the build-isolation enforcement mechanism that child 08a8-b04e-ae59-4be4 (which lands first) will have established. If 6168 restructures the python -m build call or adjusts pyproject.toml build config without explicitly preserving --no-build-isolation or the Hatchling pin-in-requires, the free-resolution remainder vector that 08a8 closes can be silently reopened.
findings.location: child 6168-df06-1e08-4ea3 (Scope: release.yml, pyproject.toml); child 08a8-b04e-ae59-4be4 (AC: Hatchling version enforced end-to-end, workflow lint/test step fails if enforcing mechanism is removed)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 6168's AC: '[ ] The pyproject.toml [build-system] Hatchling pin and/or --no-build-isolation flag established by Finding 4 (08a8) are preserved after this story's changes; the workflow lint step that fails if the enforcing mechanism is absent continues to pass.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Strengthen AC #3 to enumerate the required write paths (mutate, compact, push, reconcile) and require a deterministic in-session check (e.g., a test or grep assertion) confirming each path invokes the compatibility reader before any side effect.
findings.finding: AC #3 ('verified by the reader existing and being wired into the write paths') does not name which write paths constitute sufficient wiring, nor does it specify a deterministic in-session check (test command, CI job, or code assertion) that proves all required paths are covered — leaving 'wired' open to interpretation between executing agents.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 3: 'The store-compatibility gate (Finding 2) is present in the tree that ships as v1.0 (verified by the reader existing and being wired into the write paths)'
findings.suggested_fix: Revise AC #3 to: 'The store-compatibility gate (Finding 2) is present in the tree that ships as v1.0: a compatibility reader is invoked before any mutate, compact, push, and reconcile side effect (verified by passing tests that assert each path fails closed on a present-but-incompatible record and passes on a compatible record).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC #5, assign explicit section ownership for docs/migrations.md (e.g., absent-record upgrade/rollback path → Finding 2; removed-input migration messages → Finding 5) and clarify which content items belong in docs/migrations.md vs. docs/releasing.md.
findings.finding: The AC names both docs/releasing.md and docs/migrations.md as targets for multiple content items, but section-ownership is only assigned for docs/releasing.md (pinning → Finding 4; build-once → Finding 3; authorization → Finding 1). docs/migrations.md's ownership and content scope per child story are unspecified, creating a risk of duplication or omission across the three release stories that all touch releasing.md.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — bullet 5: 'docs/releasing.md and docs/migrations.md are updated to describe the new release-authorization gate, the build-once artifact flow, and the store compatibility record'
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC #5 into two bullets: one for docs/releasing.md (release-authorization gate → Finding 1; build-once flow → Finding 3; pinning → Finding 4) and one for docs/migrations.md (store compatibility record and absent-record upgrade/rollback path → Finding 2; removed-input migration messages → Finding 5).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign explicit per-story section ownership (or a single owning story) for `docs/migrations.md` in the Sequencing section, mirroring the per-story ownership already declared for `docs/releasing.md`.
findings.finding: The Sequencing section assigns per-story section ownership for `docs/releasing.md` (pinning→Finding 4; build-once→Finding 3; authorization→Finding 1) to prevent overlap, but `docs/migrations.md` — which AC item 5 requires to be updated — has no corresponding ownership or serialization assignment. If Finding 2 and another story both touch `docs/migrations.md`, the file-impact serialization guarantee the plan relies on is absent for that file.
findings.location: Sequencing section + AC item 5
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Sequencing section: '`docs/migrations.md` is owned by Finding 2 (store compatibility record, absent-record upgrade/rollback path) and Finding 5 (removed-input migration messages); assign non-overlapping sections and record file-impact so `next-batch` serialises them.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'reader' in AC item 3 with the terminology used in the plan body (e.g. 'the compatibility-check logic / gate' or introduce 'reader' as a named component consistently across Context, Success Criteria, and AC).
findings.finding: AC item 3 introduces the term 'reader' ('verified by the reader existing and being wired into the write paths') to describe the store-compatibility gate component, but this term does not appear anywhere else in the plan — Context, Success Criteria, and body all use 'gate', 'record', 'store-format-version / required-capability record', and 'ensure-registry'. The AC's verify step references an entity ('reader') not named in the deliverable description.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC item 3's verification clause to: 'verified by the compatibility gate existing as a callable component and being invoked on all write/mutate/compact/push/reconcile paths'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a mandatory RED-fixture or integration-test probe to the Finding 2 child story that runs a pre-v1.0 binary against a store containing the new compatibility record and asserts the binary operates without error, before the rollback-safe guarantee is encoded in release documentation.
findings.finding: The plan asserts rollback safety for Finding 2 ('the record is an ignored-unknown file to old binaries') without mandating any empirical probe — a spike, a RED fixture, or an integration test — that runs a pre-v1.0 binary against a store carrying the new compatibility record to verify the binary truly ignores the file and operates correctly. This is a behavioral claim about existing code whose correctness cannot be established by reasoning alone.
findings.location: Context — Finding 2 description; Success Criteria bullet 2; AC line 4
findings.suggested_fix: In the Finding 2 child story's acceptance criteria, require: (1) a parameterized fixture that plants the new `store-format` record in a test store and invokes the pre-v1.0 binary's read and write code paths, asserting no crash/rejection; (2) CI must execute this fixture and pass before the story receives `Verified +1`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Finding 2 and Finding 5 child stories that format-version values, required-capability identifiers, and removed-input tombstone messages are each declared in a single, clearly named location (e.g., a `store_compat.py` constants module and a `_REMOVED_INPUTS` table), so future maintainers have one place to update per change.
findings.finding: The store-compatibility record (Finding 2) introduces a format-version number and a required-capabilities registry that are expected to evolve with each future breaking release, yet the plan does not address whether these values will be centralised in a single versioned manifest or scattered as hardcoded literals across the write-path wiring points. Similarly, Finding 5's tombstone registry (the list of removed env-var and TOML keys with their migration messages) will grow over time but its storage structure is unspecified.
findings.location: Context — Finding 2 (format version, required capabilities); Finding 5 (removed-input tombstones); Success Criteria bullets 2 and 5
findings.suggested_fix: In each child story's description, add a design note: 'All format-version constants, capability identifiers, and tombstone entries MUST be declared in a single authoritative module/table; write-path wiring imports from that module rather than re-declaring literals.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before writing the Finding 4 child story, verify that `modelcontextprotocol/registry` publishes stable per-version `mcp-publisher` releases with a downloadable checksum manifest or SLSA provenance, and record the pinning mechanism (versioned URL + `sha256sum` check, or alternative) in the child's description.
findings.finding: Finding 4 (SHA-pin the release toolchain) makes an unverified capability assertion: the Success Criteria and plan text specify that `mcp-publisher` (a binary from `modelcontextprotocol/registry`) will be 'exact-versioned + digest-verified,' but the plan does not establish whether that project actually publishes stable, versioned binary releases with a corresponding checksum manifest (e.g., a `checksums.txt` or SLSA provenance) that would make digest-pinning feasible.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'an exact-versioned + digest-verified MCP publisher'; .github/workflows/release.yml:129 (current releases/latest/download fetch)
findings.suggested_fix: Check https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/releases for a versioned binary and an accompanying `checksums.txt` or `mcp-publisher_checksums.txt`. If present, the child story should use a pinned version URL and verify against that digest (analogous to `.github/workflows/external-integration.yml:90`'s `sha256sum -c --strict` pattern already in the repo). If not present, consider vendoring the binary or using a container image by digest.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] The Finding 2 story must assert the post-rollback committed-store STATE explicitly: name the record's filename/path, confirm it is either covered by a `.gitignore` entry that pre-v1.0 code already honours or prove that pre-v1.0 compact/fsck/reconcile silently skips unknown committed files — and add this as a verifiable AC (not just a prose claim that it is 'ignored').
findings.finding: The plan describes a 'committed store compatibility record' but does not assert the committed state of the store AFTER a rollback to a pre-v1.0 binary — it only says old binaries 'ignore' the file. Because the record is committed (not git-ignored), it persists in the store's git history and working tree after a binary rollback. The plan does not confirm whether old-code operations (compaction, fsck, reconcile) are safe when an unknown committed file is present, nor whether the record's filename/path is covered by existing `.gitignore` patterns so old code does not attempt to process or remove it.
findings.location: Context / Finding 2 paragraph; Success Criteria bullet 2; AC line 'The Finding 2 story defines and documents... a rollback-safe outcome for a pre-v1.0 binary encountering the record.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC to the Finding 2 child story: 'A pre-v1.0 binary booted against a store that already contains the compat record (a) does not error, (b) does not remove or corrupt the record file, and (c) leaves the store in a state where a subsequent v1.0 upgrade requires no manual repair — verified by an integration test that writes the record with a v1.0 code path, then exercises compact/fsck with pre-record code paths (or a documented proof that these paths skip all unrecognised committed files).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before committing the Finding 4 child story, verify that `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry` publishes a per-release checksum artifact (e.g. `checksums.txt` or `.sha256`) alongside the `mcp-publisher` tarball; if it does not, specify the alternative verification mechanism (cosign/sigstore, or downgrade to version-pin-only with an explicit rationale) in the child story's description.
findings.finding: The plan's Success Criteria for Finding 4 asserts the mcp-publisher binary must be 'exact-versioned + digest-verified', but neither the plan nor the repo confirms that `github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry` publishes a checksum file (e.g. `.sha256` or a `checksums.txt`) alongside its release tarballs — the mechanism required to verify the downloaded binary's digest before executing it in a privileged OIDC context. If the upstream release does not publish a checksum artifact, the digest-verification step cannot be implemented as described without a cosign/sigstore-based alternative.
findings.location: Success Criteria (Finding 4) / release.yml Install mcp-publisher step (line 127-129)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC to Finding 4 (and to this epic's AC list) requiring that the mcp-publisher binary download pins an exact release version (not `latest`) AND verifies a published checksum/digest before execution, matching the 'exact-versioned + digest-verified' language already in the Success Criteria.
findings.finding: The plan's Success Criteria declares 'an exact-versioned + digest-verified MCP publisher' but this requirement is NOT carried through to any Acceptance Criterion — none of the ACs explicitly require that the mcp-publisher binary downloaded via `curl releases/latest` is hash-verified before execution in the id-token:write context. Finding 4's description says 'SHA-pins actions, build tools, and a privileged OIDC-context binary' but the AC language only closes the loop on GitHub Actions SHA-pinning, leaving the binary-integrity contract for mcp-publisher underdeclared.
findings.location: Success Criteria ('exact-versioned + digest-verified MCP publisher') vs. Acceptance Criteria (no AC for binary hash verification); .github/workflows/release.yml lines 127-134 (mcp_registry job curl fetch)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Finding 4 story, add an AC such as: 'The mcp-publisher binary is fetched at a pinned tag URL (not releases/latest) and its SHA-256 digest is verified against a committed or registry-published checksum before execution.' Update release.yml to pin the version (e.g. MCPPUB_VERSION=0.x.y) and add a `sha256sum --check` step after the curl download.

findings.finding: Finding 2 introduces a fail-closed store compatibility gate, but the plan never specifies the behavior when the compatibility record is ABSENT (i.e., stores created by pre-v1.0 builds). If v1.0 treats 'no record present' the same as 'unknown format', every existing development user's store becomes unreadable with no rollback or migration path.
findings.location: Finding 2 / Success Criteria bullet 2 / AC line 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit policy: if no record is present, v1.0 treats the store as format-version 0 (the implicit pre-v1.0 schema), writes the record on the next mutating operation, and documents a `rebar store migrate` or equivalent escape hatch. Record this as part of the expand-contract sequence in the child story.

findings.finding: Finding 2's Success Criteria specifies fail-closed behavior for 'unknown format version or required capability' but never states how the gate behaves when the compatibility record is ABSENT — which is the state of EVERY pre-v1.0 store after the upgrade. If absence is treated as fail-closed, the v1.0 binary cannot be used on any existing store until the record is explicitly written (e.g. via init/fsck --repair), breaking the upgrade path silently. If absence is treated as compatible (fail-open), a store that predates the record bypasses the gate entirely, which is the exact scenario the gate is meant to prevent. This is a design decision, not an implementation detail, and must be stated in the plan.
findings.location: Success Criteria (Finding 2 bullet) / AC bullet 3 ('the store-compatibility gate is present in the tree that ships as v1.0')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a clause to the Finding 2 Success Criteria such as: 'An absent record (pre-v1.0 store) is treated as format version 0 / compatible; the first covered write by a v1.0 binary writes the record (via the ensure-registry or init), converting the store to the versioned state. This ensures upgrade-in-place works without manual intervention.'

findings.finding: Finding 2 introduces a committed store-format-version record into existing stores, but the plan does not specify the expand phase: how v1.0 first writes this record to already-initialized stores (pre-v1.0 stores have no such record). The Success Criteria jump directly to fail-closed enforcement without describing the record-creation path for stores that pre-date v1.0.
findings.location: Finding 2 / Success Criteria ('A v1.0 binary reads a committed store compatibility record and fails closed...')
findings.suggested_fix: Add an ensure unit (School B, idempotent) that writes the compatibility record at the current version if absent, and runs at init/fsck-repair/MCP-boot. The fail-closed gate then only fires when the record is present but incompatible — absent means 'needs migration', which the pending-hint surfaces. Document this 'absent = needs migration, not blocked' vs 'present but incompatible = blocked' distinction in the AC.

findings.finding: Child 21dd-7a0b-2119-4473 specifies that the compatibility record is 'created at init' and that the gate runs before mutations, but does not define the behavior when the gate encounters an existing store that was initialized before this feature — creating an ambiguous and potentially breaking upgrade path for all current rebar users.
findings.location: child 21dd-7a0b-2119-4473 — Acceptance Criteria AC1, AC2; What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'A store initialized without a compatibility record (pre-v1.0) is treated as format_version=1 with empty required_capabilities (pass-through), and the gate auto-writes the record on the next init/migration run; this behavior is documented in docs/migrations.md.'

findings.finding: AC5 defers a judgment call to the executor without a decision rule: 'the ones that genuinely improve the plan applied' provides no stated criterion for what constitutes a qualifying improvement, leaving the executor to determine this subjectively.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5: 'The plan-review advisory findings on each child story have been reviewed and the ones that genuinely improve the plan applied.'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC5 with: 'Each child story's plan-review advisory findings are recorded with an explicit Accept/Reject disposition and a one-line rationale (committed to the story or its commit message); accepted findings are reflected in the story text before the Gerrit +1 votes are cast.'

findings.finding: Parent AC5 ('The plan-review advisory findings on each child story have been reviewed and the ones that genuinely improve the plan applied') is not covered by any child's acceptance criteria — it is a meta/process criterion that exists only at the parent level with no child-level verifiable gate.
findings.location: Parent acceptance criteria — AC5; no child story
findings.suggested_fix: Add a lightweight child task or a documented review artifact (e.g., a Gerrit CL comment block listing each advisory finding and its disposition) so AC5 is machine-checkable at parent closure.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
